svg.title: Pipeline OS v2 — 13 stages from concept to telemetry
svg.desc: A 13-stage educational game pipeline. New stages added after Echo Heist and Orbital Drift are marked with a star and colored teal.
svg.text: idea → kill test → fantasy → spec → contract → build → audit → telemetry

g.text: Raw idea, loop sentence, role, consequence
Grade band, CCSS, cognitive load, Bartle type
Role must require the math, not merely permit it
Feedback states and a11y locked before visuals
Signature moment must be nameable and concrete
Problem before instruction, concrete before symbol
Every particle, sound, shake, easing, signature beat
Forbids regressions, locks fonts, forbids hidden actives
Predict errors, map telemetry, draft teacher language

svg.text: Hard rejection rules
1. Role does not require the math → reject, not revise
2. Loop cannot be said in one sentence with a world change → reject
3. World does not visibly break on a wrong answer → reject
4. Player can guess their way to success → reject
5. K–2 reflection requires reading → reject build, revise spec
6. Any screen loads with zero visible interactive elements → reject build
7. Feel audit scores below 16 of 20 → reject build
Why each new stage exists
Stage 2 — Echo Heist almost shipped a guessable lock concept
Stage 3 — Thief fantasy did not naturally require math
Stage 6 — Orbital Drift needed Brilliant-style per-state feedback
Stage 7 — Signature moment must be authored before code
Stage 9 — Star Courier v1 regressed UX on rebuild, nothing caught it
Stage 11 — Feel scored post-hoc, now graded against 20 criteria
Structural shift
Old pipeline: stages 0–6 funnel toward a prototype, build is the main event
New pipeline: stages 0–9 are the real work, build only executes locked decisions
No code begins until multisensory_spec.json and ux_contract.json are signed off
New stage added in v2
Carried over from v1
